gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: delegateClaim has no role check at Portal dispatch layer; roller implementation access control unverifiable from repo source
candidate.claimed_root_cause: Portal.delegateClaim applies only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH) before delegatecalling PORTAL_ROLLER; no ROLLER_ROLE or DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE check exists at the Portal layer; roller source is absent from the repository
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged EOA triggers LP fee collection and distribution to _tokenBeneficiaries[token] without authorization, constituting unauthorized reward extraction

live_preconditions.name: CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH is set (protocol not halted)
live_preconditions.observed_value: Protocol is live with 1361+ BNB balance at block 108382650; halt not indicated

live_preconditions.name: At least one token with non-zero _tokenBeneficiaries entry and claimable LP fees exists
live_preconditions.observed_value: Not directly observed; BeneficiaryClaimed events expected on live chain

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_ROLLER implementation address is non-zero
live_preconditions.observed_value: Not directly observed; PORTAL_ROLLER defined in PortalBase.sol but value not readable from indexed source

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_ROLLER implementation source has no caller access control on delegateClaim
live_preconditions.observed_value: ROLLER_ROLE constant absent from all indexed .sol files; roller implementation contract not present in repository; cannot confirm or deny
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: Decompile or obtain verified source for PORTAL_ROLLER address; check for onlyRole, require(hasRole(...)), or msg.sender checks in delegateClaim selector

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Only guard is onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH); no role check before _delegateToImpl(PORTAL_ROLLER)
Unconditional delegatecall to impl; all access control must be inside the roller implementation
_tokenBeneficiaries mapping at slot 169 is the reward destination; roller reads this in Portal's storage context
Documents 'Only the roller or default admin can call this function' — intended restriction not enforced at Portal.sol dispatch layer
GUARDIAN_ROLE, TAX_MANAGER_ROLE, TAX_GUARDIAN_ROLE, TOKEN_FLAP_FEE_SETTER_ROLE, MODERATOR_ROLE defined; ROLLER_ROLE absent entirely from all indexed source

attacker_path.actor: Arbitrary unprivileged EOA with no roles
attacker_inputs: token: any launched token address whose _tokenBeneficiaries[token] is non-zero and has unclaimed LP fees
call_sequence: 3. _delegateToImpl(PORTAL_ROLLER) executes roller's delegateClaim in Portal's storage context
4. If roller has no caller check: LP fees collected and sent to _tokenBeneficiaries[token]
state_before: _tokenBeneficiaries[token] is set to a known address
state_after: Attacker triggered the distribution without authorization

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: LP fees (BNB or quote tokens) accumulated for a launched token
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Attacker can trigger fee distribution to beneficiary at will; if attacker controls the beneficiary address, attacker receives LP fees; if not, attacker can force premature/repeated distribution
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: LP fee accounting integrity; beneficiary entitlement timing; potential for repeated claims if roller lacks idempotency guard
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker has no role entitlement to trigger delegateClaim per NatSpec; if attacker controls beneficiary, they receive fees without being the authorized roller or admin

setup: Query BeneficiaryClaimed events to find a token with known beneficiary and claimable fees
transaction_sequence: vm.prank(address(0xdead)); // arbitrary EOA with no roles
expected_assertions: If call reverts with any error: REJECT — roller enforces access control
If call succeeds and beneficiary balance increases: HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE — roller has no caller check
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: Call reverts (roller enforces onlyRole or equivalent check internally)

rejection_reason: Cannot be promoted beyond NEEDS_LOCAL_PROOF because the PORTAL_ROLLER implementation contract source is absent from the repository. The critical gate not_external_dependency_only is false: the entire access-control question depends on whether the roller implementation (an external contract not in scope source) enforces a caller check. ROLLER_ROLE is confirmed absent from all indexed .sol files, which is consistent with either (a) the roller using a different guard such as DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE or a hardcoded address check, or (b) no guard at all. A BSC fork test calling delegateClaim from an unprivileged EOA is the only way to resolve this binary.
